title: Per-event orchestrator transition log row
description: Schema for a single JSONL row in docs/plans/<slug>/_orchestrator_log.jsonl. Per implplan §C.impl.3. Schema-version trajectory: 1 (v1.0-v1.2), 2 (v1.3-revised), 3 (v1.4-revised), 4 (v1.5-audit-response), 5 (§G.impl ship — adds bin/hook-log + bin/log + bin/security-dispatch + bin/hook-lint + 2 sub-emitters to KNOWN_WRITERS). All five versions accepted by SUPPORTED_VERSIONS_LOG; closed-enum surfaces are the same shape across versions. Forward-compat per implplan §B.impl.3a: unknown leading-underscore fields are tolerated.

EmittedBy.description: Validation against KNOWN_WRITERS happens at write time in bin/_jsonl_log/writer.py; schema-level constraint is non-empty string only because re-validating the closed enum in the schema would require regenerating this file each time KNOWN_WRITERS gains an entry. The implplan §C.impl.6 contract is the source of truth.

event_type.description: Optional payload-level discriminator for sub-classifying transition rows. Recognized values include: overnight_rollback_override_used, iteration_overflow_sentinel, develop_plan_status_mapped, develop_plan_rollback_refused, state_md_rendered (orch_status_render T4), state_md_render_failed (orch_status_render T4), chain_paused (CCOR.1 T-5), chain_resumed (CCOR.1 T-6), chain_paused_lock_stale_cleared (CCOR.1 T-7), pause_inject_consumed (CCOR.1 T-7). The list is illustrative, not enforced; new event_types may be added by writers without schema bump. Free-form string per the additive philosophy documented at bin/_update_orchestrator/log_emit.py lines 90-98.

RecoveryRow.description: Schema branch for `_corrupt_truncated` audit rows emitted by _validate_or_truncate_last_line. Per implplan §C.impl.8: session_id is fixed `_recovery`; mode_at_transition allows nulls only on this branch; transition.from/to are both `unknown`; carries `_corrupt_bytes_hex` + `_truncated_byte_count` additive fields.

event_type.description: Optional payload-level discriminator (same shape as StandardRow.event_type). Recognized values mirror the StandardRow.event_type list, including the CCOR.1 additions: chain_paused, chain_resumed, chain_paused_lock_stale_cleared, pause_inject_consumed. Tolerated on RecoveryRow for forward-compat although recovery rows are not currently expected to carry this field.
